analysis.object: T804: Lattice-to-Continuum as Categorification depth analysis

lattice_sites.description: Lattice sites: discrete points Z^4 -- EML-0

lattice_links.description: Lattice links U_mu in SU(N): group elements -- EML-0 (compact group, discrete topology)

lattice_plaquette.description: Plaquette = product of 4 links: still EML-0

lattice_action.description: Wilson action S = sum exp(Tr(plaquette)): EML-1 (exponential of sum)

continuum_field.description: Continuum gauge field A_mu(x): EML-3 (continuous oscillatory)

limit_categorification.description: a -> 0 limit: EML-0 lattice -> EML-3 continuum -- Delta_d = +3 categorification
limit_categorification.reason: Continuum limit IS categorification: EML-0 -> EML-3

t804_theorem.description: T804: The lattice-to-continuum limit is a Delta_d=+3 categorification. Same structure as the Hodge descent problem (but in reverse). The REVERSE descent (continuum -> lattice -> classical) is the Hodge descent pattern. T804.
t804_theorem.reason: Lattice-continuum = categorification; controlled by Hodge descent in reverse
